items.title: Προβλήματα σε Facebook, Messenger και Instagram

items.snippet: Χωρίς ρεύμα είναι από τις 15:20 και έπειτα αρκετές περιοχές στο κέντρο και κυρίως στην Κυψέλη, σε Εξάρχεια, Κολωνάκι και Γκύζη. Αρκετοί σηματοδότες έχουν τεθεί εκτός λειτουργίας, ενώ δίχως ηλεκτροδότηση είναι πολλά καταστήματα. Ταυτόχρονα, η Πυροσβεστική έχει λάβει αρκετές κλήσει

items.title: Βουλή: «Ναι» στην άρση ασυλίας Κωνσταντοπούλου και Σαλμά

items.title: Αιχμές Μητσοτάκη στην αντιπολίτευση: «Τάζουν τα πάντα, ξέρουν ότι δεν κερδίζουν τις εκλογές»
items.snippet: «Είναι πολύ εύκολο να υπόσχεσαι τα πάντα στους πάντες - Με το αζημίωτο είναι, τζάμπα είναι» δήλωσε ο πρωθυπουργός σε κοινή συνέντευξη τύπου με τον Πορτογάλο ομόλογό του.

lead.title: Expected U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Fuels Israeli Concerns of Regional Arms Race
lead.snippet: An Israeli minister sought to downplay the deal, but critics said it could spur other regional countries to pursue a nuclear program, and potentially the creation of a bomb.

items.title: Ukraine’s New Military Chief Faces a Monumental Test
items.snippet: Maj. Gen. Mykhailo Drapatyi faces a balancing act between asserting the independence favored by military reformers and accommodating political input on battlefield strategy.

items.title: A Lettuce-Growing Region in Mexico Stomachs the Fallout From the Cyclospora Outbreak
items.snippet: In central Mexico, a lettuce-growing region has been potentially linked to an outbreak in the U.S. that has sickened thousands.

items.title: Iran War Live Updates: Trump Threatens Retaliation Against Civilian Targets if Iran Strikes in Strait
items.snippet: President Trump said the United States would target a bridge or power plant, including in or near the Iranian capital, if Iran fired on the Strait of Hormuz. Secretary of State Marco Rubio earlier suggested that Tehran was “not serious” about diplomacy.

items.title: Japan Is Startled by Prime Minister’s Grueling Sleep Schedule
items.snippet: Sanae Takaichi has said she gets just “zero to three hours” of sleep a night, prompting a debate in the country over work-life balance.

items.title: Firing of Ukraine’s Top General Signals the End of an Era
items.snippet: Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi, who commanded crucial battles, oversaw an expansion of drone warfare against Russia, but many Ukrainians want even more.

items.title: Iran’s Neighbors Long for a Deal, Any Deal, to End the War
items.snippet: Business owners in Pakistan are getting impatient for a chance to finally trade goods and oil along their long shared border with Iran.

items.title: After Decades of Caution, Germany Is Shaking Up How It Makes Weapons
items.snippet: As threats from Moscow rise and support from Washington dwindles, the German military is reinventing how it researches, funds and wields 21st-century arms.

items.title: Inside the Fight to Rid South Africa of Black Immigrants
items.snippet: As immigration surges in the country, activists are going to extreme lengths to expel foreign nationals, often taking the law into their own hands.

items.title: 3 Myths Fueling Anti-Immigrant Sentiment in South Africa
items.snippet: As the nation convulses with protests and violence, data and research suggest that some of the biggest arguments against foreigners don’t stand up against the facts.

items.title: K-pop’s NewJeans Hints at Comeback After Monthslong Feud With Its Label
items.snippet: The girl group had tried to cut ties with Ador, a subsidiary of the behemoth Hybe, but a court squashed that effort.

items.title: India’s Young Protesters Are Bloodied but Unbowed
items.snippet: A day after police forcibly broke up demonstrations, thousands returned to the protest site in New Delhi to keep pushing for action over the country’s failings in education.

items.title: Netanyahu Ad Uses a Questionable Tactic Against Bereaved Rival
items.snippet: A.I.-generated images showing Gadi Eisenkot, the Israeli leader’s top challenger in upcoming elections, played on anti-Arab sentiments and used images seen as disrespectful to his son, who was killed in the recent Gaza war.

items.title: The Teenage Boys In Search of Their Mother After the Venezuela Earthquake
items.snippet: After Venezuela’s devastating earthquakes, Winder and Deivi Delfín dug every day for 26 days to find the bodies of their mother, brother and grandmother.

items.title: Trump’s 50% Tariffs on Canada: What to Know, and What’s Next
items.snippet: The president said punishing 50 percent tariffs would begin by Aug. 19, a significant escalation of the trade war between Canada and the U.S. that he started over a year ago.

lead.title: The browser wars aren’t about search anymore — here are the best alternatives to Chrome and Safari
lead.snippet: We’ve compiled an overview of some of the top alternative browsers available today aiming to challenge Chrome and Safari.

items.title: Passionfroot raises $15M to expand its B2B creator marketplace to the US
items.snippet: Passionfroot, a German startup building a marketplace connecting B2B creators with brands, has raised $15M in a Series A round led by Insight Partners.

items.title: WhatsApp adds Apple CarPlay and Android Auto upgrades, iPad sign-ups, and more
items.snippet: WhatsApp on Apple CarPlay and Android Auto will let users hear and reply to messages, make calls, view their call history, and access favorite contacts hands-free and right from their car's screen.

items.title: Glow emerges from stealth at $1.2B valuation to challenge endpoint security in the AI era
items.snippet: Glow is targeting a new class of endpoint risks created by the rapid adoption of AI agents and developer tools inside enterprises.

items.title: Synthesia’s AI training platform is moving beyond videos into live coaching
items.snippet: Synthesia launched AI Roleplay Sessions, an interactive enterprise training platform where employees practice workplace conversations with AI avatars that provide feedback, scoring, and analytics to help companies measure training effectiveness.

items.title: The Anthropic-Physical Intelligence rumor roiling AI Twitter
items.snippet: Anthropic and OpenAI's aggressive 2026 acquisition sprees set the stage for a weekend rumor.

items.title: Dimension Capital’s $800M third fund shows the intersection of science and compute is booming
items.snippet: The four-year-old firm's latest fund is 60% larger than its second vehicle announced 18 months ago.

items.title: Meta is testing an AI bedtime story app for people with no imagination
items.snippet: At last, a tech company has found a way to outsource humanity's oldest pastime: using our imaginations.

items.title: These are the countries moving to ban social media for children
items.snippet: Australia was the first country to issue a ban in late 2025, aiming to reduce the pressures and risks that young users may face on social media, including cyberbullying, social media addiction, and exposure to predators.

items.title: OpenAI says Hugging Face was breached by its pre-release models
items.snippet: OpenAI has come forward to claim responsibility for the Hugging Face breach, saying it was the result of internal testing gone awry.

items.title: Jack Dorsey is taking on Slack with Buzz, a group chat platform for teams and their AI agents
items.snippet: Buzz is a group chat platform for the workplace that puts humans and their AI agents in the same conversation.

items.title: AI and the rise of the universal entertainment app

items.title: Bucking EV slowdown, Sila raises $300M to expand battery materials factory
items.snippet: Sila's fresh $300 million round will help it make enough of its silicon-carbon anode material to power more than 100,000 EVs.

items.title: Einride bets $38M on EV charging as it scales electric trucking
items.snippet: The acquisition — Einride's first as a publicly traded company — will expand its EV-charging ecosystem as it works to scale its electric trucking business.

items.title: Apple teams up with Klarna to launch a lease-to-own program for iPhones, iPads, and Macs
items.snippet: The new leasing program is a big change for the hardware company, and comes as it looks to raise prices on many of its products.

items.title: Ολυμπιακός: Οι πανηγυρισμοί από τους οπαδούς της Ναϊμέχεν για την κλήρωση με τους Πειραιώτες

items.title: Παναθηναϊκός: Έντονο ενδιαφέρον του ΑΠΟΕΛ για Σιώπη, σύμφωνα με τους Κύπριους
items.snippet: Σύμφωνα με δημοσιεύματα από την Κύπρο, ο ΑΠΟΕΛ κάνει δυναμικό «μπάσιμο» για την απόκτηση του Μανώλη Σιώπη.

items.title: 12 πράγματα που κάναμε τα καλοκαίρια όσοι μεγαλώσαμε στα '00s (και δεν θα ξεχάσουμε ποτέ)
items.snippet: Τα καλοκαίρια των παιδιών που μεγαλωσαμε στα ‘00s θυμίζουν πια νοσταλγική σειρά εποχής.

lead.title: He’s the Last Great Land Artist You’ve Never Heard Of
lead.snippet: Charles Ross spent 50 years building “Star Axis,” a naked-eye observatory in New Mexico. Now his masterwork is ready. How to share it with a changed world?

items.title: Monkeys Understand Shapes in Almost the Same Way as Humans
items.snippet: Our geometric intuition, long considered something that sets us apart, seems to be shared with other primates.

items.title: Six Years After a Reckoning, Criterion Puts ‘Cinematic Blackness’ in Focus
items.snippet: Adding a curator, the Criterion Collection expanded its offerings and its vision of Black film. But auteurs continue to push the company on what inclusion means.

items.title: ‘Too Rare to Care’? A New Center for Rare Diseases Hopes to Change That
items.snippet: A new nonprofit wants to streamline gene therapy for diseases often avoided by pharmaceutical companies — making treatment more like a routine procedure than a bespoke drug.

items.title: Kenneth Blume Steps Out of Kenny Beats’s Shadow
items.snippet: The hitmaker formerly known as Kenny Beats was behind breakthrough albums for Geese, Vince Staples, Idles and Fcukers. On a new solo project, he turns his focus inward.

items.title: The Best Genetic Risk Tools Don’t Work Equally for Everyone
items.snippet: Genetic prediction models are poised to revolutionize medicine. But because they have been trained on European DNA, they threaten to widen health care disparities.

items.title: Marlboro Music: Where Artists Have Time to Chase Perfection
items.snippet: The Marlboro Music Festival, now in its 75th summer, allows artists to practice for however long they’d like, with no pressure to perform.

items.title: Trump Attacks National Academies Over Climate Chapter in Judges’ Manual
items.snippet: The president joined Republican officials who slammed the National Academies of Sciences for a chapter about climate science in an educational guide for judges.

items.title: This French Ballet Star Wants You to Know He Really Cares
items.snippet: Hugo Marchand, an étoile at Paris Opera Ballet, brings his intensity and some high-wattage friends to New York City Center’s Artists at the Center.

items.title: The Sewer Knows What You Ate
items.snippet: During the Covid-19 pandemic, wastewater testing became a virus warning system. Now, scientists are using the approach to understand how communities eat.

items.title: Tate Museums Taps Leader of Dia Art Foundation as Next Director
items.snippet: After leading the Dia Art Foundation for over a decade, Jessica Morgan is returning to London to run Tate at a challenging time for the renowned group of art museums.

items.title: As Natural Disasters Expand, a Disaster-Tracking App Is Booming
items.snippet: Known for tracking California wildfires, Watch Duty is moving beyond that mission. Millions of people are turning to it for help.

items.title: Former Conductor to Plead Guilty in Child Sex Abuse Imagery Case
items.snippet: Julian Wachner, the former music director at Trinity Church in New York, faces up to 10 years in prison after reaching a plea deal.

items.title: A Mathematical Tribute to the Soccer Ball
items.snippet: As the World Cup final approaches, take a moment to appreciate the beautiful symmetries of humanity’s favorite truncated icosahedron.

items.title: New Poster for ‘La La Land’ Fixes Ryan Gosling’s Wayward Hand
items.snippet: Gosling’s hand position during a dance scene didn’t match Emma Stone’s, haunting him for years after the movie’s release.
